The Collection

Photographic Gallery

Twelve restored portraits spanning the full arc of the Victorian and Edwardian ages — from daguerreotype to gelatin silver.

✦   ✦   ✦
Restored

Sisters

Albumen print, c.1895. A rare double portrait of quiet intimacy.

Restored

Young Woman in Peach Silk

Cabinet card, c.1888. Puffed sleeves and fitted corset bodice.

Repose at the Pedestal

Gelatin silver print, c.1900. Studio portrait with turned mahogany prop.

Restored

Portrait in Green Silk

Albumen print, c.1878. Full-length studio portrait with carved furnishings.

Restored

Repose upon the Moon

Albumen print, c.1890. Allegorical Pre-Raphaelite composition.

Girl with Auburn Coiffure

Gelatin silver print, c.1895. Pearl and gold-cross necklace.

Restored

Woman with Velvet Choker

Gelatin silver print, c.1893. Black velvet ribbon and satin bow.

Studio of John R. Stringer, Sunderland

Cabinet card, c.1898. Vignetted studio portrait in mauve silk.

Restored

Teal Velvet and Gold Chains

Gelatin silver print, c.1907. Layered Edwardian jewellery.

By the Window

Gelatin silver print, c.1905. Available-light domestic portrait.

Restored

Infant Portrait

Gelatin silver print, c.1908. Christening dress and sheepskin rug.

Mourning Dress with Gold Brooch

Gelatin silver print, c.1895. Black silk with puffed sleeves.

Restored

Portrait with White Peonies

Albumen print, c.1905. Painted landscape backdrop, high-end studio.

✦   ✦   ✦

If you have a Victorian or Edwardian photograph in need of restoration, or wish to contribute to the archive, please contact us.

The Silvered Glass Archive

A non-commercial archive dedicated to Victorian and Edwardian photographic heritage.
